text: I find myself simultaneously an observer and a participant—on one side, among people gathered for a communal experience, perhaps a lecture, palpable with anticipation and quiet reflection. On the other, I am in a space of innovation and inquiry, surrounded by vivid colors, concepts, and screens that celebrate the pursuit of knowledge and progress. Here, I feel a blend of curiosity and humility. The presence of so many minds—each with their own histories and perspectives—invites me to ponder my own presence, my own contributions within the ongoing dialogue of humanity. This is a moment to listen, to think, and ultimately, to engage with both tradition and possibility.
